summary: Linked Sheik's ftSk_SpecialLw translation unit by converting it from NonMatching to Matching and doing targeted cleanup in the source/header. The main matching tactic was replacing file-scope float constants with direct literals plus a small inline wrapper around lb_800119DC to force the required sdata2 ordering. The PR also tightened function signatures from HSD_GObj* to Fighter_GObj*, removed many redundant casts, made local helper callbacks static, and removed an obsolete efSync_Spawn comment.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/ft/chara/ftSeak/ftSk_SpecialLw.c is built as Object(Matching, ...) instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).

Updated ftSk_SpecialLw.c to use Fighter_GObj* signatures for Sheik SpecialLw entry, animation, IASA, physics, collision, and transition functions, eliminating many explicit casts from HSD_GObj* to Fighter_GObj*.
Changed helper callbacks fn_80114034, fn_801140B0, and fn_8011412C to static Fighter_GObj* functions, reflecting local linkage and fighter-specific object typing.

Removed named file-scope float constants such as ftSk_Init_804D9698, ftSk_Init_804D969C, ftSk_Init_804D96A0, ftSk_Init_804D96A4, and ftSk_Init_804D96A8, replacing most uses with direct literals like 0.0F and 1.0F.

Added <math.h> and introduced static helper ftSk_unk_inline(Vec3*) that calls lb_800119DC(v, 0x78, 0.4F, 0.003F, M_PI / 3), described in the PR body as an inline function to force proper sdata2 ordering.
Updated motion-state calls to pass direct constants such as 0xC4C508E, 1.0F, and 0.0F instead of the removed static float constants.
Updated ground/air comparison in ftSk_SpecialLw_80114758 from a raw integer comparison to fp->ground_or_air == GA_Ground.
Updated ftSk_SpecialLw.h to include <melee/ft/forward.h> and changed exported prototypes from HSD_GObj* to Fighter_GObj*.
Removed a stale comment from src/melee/ef/efsync.h about a suspected efSync_Spawn call shape in fn_80114034.
smart_moves: Used an inline wrapper around lb_800119DC rather than preserving artificial file-scope float constants, allowing the code to stay cleaner while still controlling sdata2 ordering for matching.
Switched APIs and headers to Fighter_GObj* consistently, which removes noisy casts and better documents that these callbacks operate on fighter game objects.
Marked internal accessory callback functions static, reducing unnecessary header/API surface while matching local usage.
Replaced magic raw ground/air integer check with GA_Ground, improving semantic clarity without changing behavior.
Removed obsolete explanatory comments once the matching call shape was resolved, keeping efsync.h focused on the actual declaration.
decomp_lessons: When a translation unit fails due to small-data ordering, a small static inline helper can be a useful matching lever; in this case ftSk_unk_inline centralized the lb_800119DC call and used M_PI / 3 to replace a hand-written 1.04719758 constant.
Prefer Fighter_GObj* for fighter action-state callbacks when callers and callees are fighter-specific; this can eliminate casts and still match.
Do not keep synthetic static float constants merely because the decompiler emitted them if direct literals or expressions produce the required codegen and better sdata2 layout.
Header prototypes should be updated in lockstep with source signatures and may need the proper forward header, here <melee/ft/forward.h>, when moving from HSD_GObj* to Fighter_GObj*.
Small cleanup in adjacent headers, such as removing stale efSync_Spawn speculation, is appropriate when the matching source no longer needs that uncertainty documented there.
naming_conventions: Sheik files and symbols continue to use the ftSk_ prefix, e.g. ftSk_SpecialLw_Enter, ftSk_SpecialAirLw_Enter, ftSk_SpecialLw2_Anim, and ftSk_SpecialLw_80114758.
Action-state comments use descriptive names such as Sheik_AS_361_Transform_Grounded, Sheik_AS_363_Transform_Aerial, Animation_SheikTransformStartGround, and AS_SheikFinishTransformation.
Unidentified local helper retained an unknown-style name, ftSk_unk_inline, rather than inventing an unsupported semantic name.
Internal address-like helper names such as fn_80114034 and fn_801140B0 were kept but made static because evidence only supports local callback behavior.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed ftSk_SpecialLw.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py.
Used static inline-like helper ftSk_unk_inline(Vec3*) to call lb_800119DC with 0x78, 0.4F, 0.003F, and M_PI / 3, explicitly noted by the author as needed to force proper sdata2 ordering.
Removed file-scope float constants and used direct literals in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls, including 0.0F, 1.0F, attributes->x70, and fp->cur_anim_frame.
Reduced cast noise by making functions accept Fighter_GObj* directly and by passing fp directly to ftCommon_8007D494 and ftCommon_8007CEF4.
Used enum constant GA_Ground instead of raw 0 in a ground_or_air branch.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states the motivation: link ftSk_SpecialLw with minor GObj cast cleanups and an inline function to force proper sdata2 ordering.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that used inline helper functions to force sdata2 ordering.
Search for remaining ftSeak files using HSD_GObj* where Fighter_GObj* would be type-correct.
Search for file-scope float constants in matching fighter files that may be replaceable with literals or math expressions.
Review other Sheik/Zelda transform code around ftZd_SpecialLw_8013B4D8 and accessory4_cb for similar cleanup opportunities.
